title: Subscribe to Health Canada’s Hospital Mandatory Reporting Newsletter - Canada.ca
content:
## Subscribe to Health Canada’s Hospital Mandatory Reporting Newsletter
- About the newsletter
- Subscribe to the newsletter
- Change your subscription
The personal information you provide to Health  Canada is:
- handled in accordance with the Privacy Act
- used by the Health Products Surveillance and Epidemiology Bureau under the authority of the Food and Drug Regulations
Why we collect your personal information
The Canada Vigilance Program will use your personal information to implement an email subscription list of  hospital stakeholders, to distribute periodic newsletters,  notifications concerning mandatory reporting and webinar invitations.
How else we use or share your personal information
The Canada Vigilance Program may share your personal information with other programs within Health Canada that support  mandatory reporting by hospitals.
If you don't want to provide  your personal information
If you choose not to provide your email address, we  won't be able to send you updates.
You have the right to:
- access and request corrections and notations to your personal information
- complain to the Privacy Commissioner of Canada if you feel your personal information has been handled improperly
Contact us for more information about these rights,  or about how we handle your personal information:
Email: canadavigilance@hc-sc.gc.ca .

### For more information:
Info Source describes the collection of your  personal information at infosource.gc.ca . Refer to the personal information bank  (PIB) HC PSU 914 Public Communications .

## About the newsletter
Health Canada’s Hospital Mandatory Reporting Newsletter is published quarterly by the Canada Vigilance Program . The newsletter shares information with hospitals about the requirements for reporting medical device incidents and serious adverse drug reactions to Health Canada.
The newsletter provides topics of interest on :
- health product safety vigilance,
- statistics on mandatory reporting by hospitals,
- tips for improving the quality of reports,
- answers to questions frequently asked by hospitals.

## Subscribe to the newsletter
As part of your subscription, you will also receive webinar invitations and relevant policy and regulatory updates on mandatory reporting.
To subscribe to the newsletter, please answer the questions below and click on the " Subscribe " button.
Required fields are marked with an asterisk ( * ).

## Change your subscription
To change your subscription email, you must unsubscribe under the previous email address and re-subscribe with the new email address.
If you wish to unsubscribe, type in your email address in the box below, then select the " Unsubscribe " button.
